---
title: "Meet the Agents"
subtitle: "The Research Roles Behind the Skills"
---

# How Agents Work in Codex

The Codex version keeps the same research roles as the Claude version, but they are now activated through skills and optional sub-agent delegation rather than through a `.claude/agents/` runtime.

The active role summary lives in `plugins/clo-author/references/agent-catalog.md`. Detailed prompt copies from the Claude-era implementation are preserved under `plugins/clo-author/references/legacy-agents/`.

# Discovery Roles

## Librarian

Finds and synthesizes literature, builds annotated bibliographies, maps the frontier, and positions the project.

## librarian-critic

Checks the literature review for recency gaps, missing seminal papers, one-sided coverage, and poor positioning.

## Explorer

Builds a ranked inventory of feasible datasets and evaluates coverage, access, sample size, and fit to the research question.

## explorer-critic

Stress-tests the data recommendation on measurement validity, representativeness, and identification fit.

# Strategy Roles

## Strategist

Designs the identification strategy, estimator, robustness plan, and falsification logic.

## strategist-critic

Reviews assumptions, inference, estimator choice, and whether a skeptical referee would accept the design.

# Execution Roles

## Data-engineer

Handles cleaning, joins, panel construction, variable creation, and output plumbing.

## Coder

Implements estimators, robustness checks, tables, and figures in R, Stata, Python, or Julia.

## coder-critic

Reviews reproducibility, code quality, path safety, strategy alignment, and output sanity.

## Writer

Drafts and revises manuscript sections, translating results into empirical-paper prose.

## writer-critic

Checks notation, hedging, unsupported claims, and LaTeX quality.

# Peer Review Roles

## Editor

Runs desk review, assigns referees, and synthesizes the editorial decision.

## domain-referee

Reviews contribution, positioning, literature coverage, and substantive claims.

## methods-referee

Reviews identification, inference, robustness, and replication readiness.

# Presentation Roles

## Storyteller

Builds talks derived directly from the paper.

## storyteller-critic

Checks pacing, density, visual hierarchy, and fidelity to the paper.

# Infrastructure Roles

## Verifier

Runs compile, execution, and package-audit checks.

## Orchestrator

Coordinates phases, delegates roles, enforces quality gates, and escalates when pairs fail to converge.
